text: End-to-end demonstration of code generation and chatbot creation in the Blawx prototype I've been working on. The LLM generates a block-based, visual, domain-specific constraint logic program based on the text of the law and the ontology defined by the user, then you encode a question and an interview and you have a chatbot that doesn't have the black box problem of LLMs, but has the same easy interface. Very interested in anyone's comments.

The LLM generates a block-based, visual, domain-specific constraint logic program based on the text of the law and the ontology defined by the user, then you encode a question and an interview and you have a chatbot that doesn't have the black box problem of LLMs, but has the same easy interface. text: How do you push changes to consumers? Suppose I'm updating a function and want the new one used downstream? Do you plan some sort of push or pull mechanism?

elements.text: How do you push changes to consumers? Suppose I'm updating a function and want the new one used downstream? Do you plan some sort of push or pull mechanism?

text:
Why do you want the new one used downstream? because if you want other people to change their code I am not keen to facilitate that within the language.
However I am thinking there should be a way to broadcast information about changes. potentiall something like an @library syntax that is a moveable reference. though it still resolves to a hash that is then captured by any hashes you derive for your program
